response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with strong testing culture and some high-throughput systems exposure, but he does not meet the core minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ — his C++ work is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, with most of his career in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/full-stack development. He also lacks demonstrated cloud compute platform architecture experience and formal technical-leadership tenure mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers, making him a poor fit for this specific C++-centric platform lead role despite his general seniority.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot background with Kubernetes, Docker, and AWS exposure across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
concerns: Far short of the required 8 years of C++ experience (roughly under 1 year professionally)
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale distributed infrastructure ownership at architectural level
No formal technical-lead role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment for a platform team

key_factors.factor: High-scale systems and mentoring track record
